0xdea4af2d41d0dbcf0bef810e249cee487cfd632fa4a2d4020c794f8496d958d5
and receives 6.62 ETH from Coinbase
0xcae8398f6c83d8348503259ce0123f160c7f269c6419790dd3a99ae92ade63ff

(there are more examples but I believe enough evidence has been shown)

7/  Before this was posted I reached out to TyroneJKD on Feb 12 to confront him about receiving the funds from the 0xCube scam. 

He replied claiming he ‘just did the art and received mint funds’

I asked additional questions but did not hear back.

8/  I find it very odd:

 >He funded the contract owners wallet address

> Can directly link him to being largest recipient of mint funds (not including funds directly off-ramped)

>After I confronted him last week the remaining funds were transferred to Coinbase and he stopped receiving mint funds to his public address

>Waited 540+ days to send the 0xCube funds from Tornado to his address

>Did not publicly mention on X/Twitter he did the art

>Some of mint funds were used to bid his own art in alt wallets

>Tornado/CEX activity is very similar (instantly withdraw funds after deposit)

description: @zachxbt: 1/ An investigation into how the influencer Crypto Rover ghosted a project he was paid to promote, mislead followers about his trading positions, and also his shills for pump and dump meme coins. 2/ In May...…
fullText:
1/  An investigation into how the influencer Crypto Rover ghosted a project he was paid to promote, mislead followers about his trading positions, and also his shills for pump and dump meme coins.

2/  In May 2023 Rover was connected with a project was connected to help promote it. 

During negotiations Rover said he can “pump projects from 1/2m to 10m easy”

They agreed on $10K + 1% of the supply for payment

Rover address
0x4472d6969c0750dd7ba8e387d2b007a80794802f

3/  After the payment was sent to Rover was where things started to go poorly. 

On multiple occasions Rover agreed he was going to start promoting the project with posts but never did.

4/  Eventually Rover stopped responding. 

They become upset due to him not keeping up his end of the agreement and asked him to refund.

5/  One month later he responded claiming he did not scam and cannot refund but will make a post in better market conditions and will take legal action if they call him out. 

It’s now February 2024 and no posts have been made.

6/  To verify this address was Rover’s I mapped out the txns & found it tied to this post by matching the amounts.

He said

“I will hold this throughout the next bull run. I bet I can make an easy $1m from this investment account”

Funny enough he sold shortly after.

7/  Ten fresh addresses connected to Rover’s Oct 2023 post and the marketing payment bought up ~9% of the $STONED supply on May 15, 2023

He made 12+ posts on his X account and Telegram channel from May 16-17, 2023. 

Their team rug pulled and has not posted since May 18, 2023.

8/  The 10 fresh addresses linked to him profited ~40 ETH from STONED with sells shortly after posts.

The addresses sent the majority of ETH profits to the same Bybit deposit address Rover has transferred funds to many times. 

Bybit deposit address
0x292d6a1e7ce8a81a0e7ae04a5a1ef967def852fb

9/  Here are examples of screenshots of Rover’s posts followed by sells from the addresses tied to him shortly after (UTC time zone)

10/  I find this behavior concerning and do not think he can just claim he does not know what he is doing.

As he stated earlier that he could “pump projects from 1/2m to 10m easy”

He is not the only influencer doing this and we as a space need to stop letting them do this time after time again to their followers.

description: @zachxbt: 1/ It’s 2024 and we are still seeing far too many teams getting SIM swapped or phished on a regular basis resulting in millions stolen. So here are some tips EVERY team should follow to secure...…
fullText:
1/  It’s 2024 and we are still seeing far too many teams getting SIM swapped or phished on a regular basis resulting in millions stolen.

So here are some tips EVERY team should follow to secure their X (Twitter) account and what to prioritize if an account becomes compromised.  



https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1694326221511794706

2/  If you are subscribed or want to purchase X Premium you are required to attach a phone number to receive a check mark. 

Once you apply for the check mark you can immediately remove the phone number after. 

If you do not remove the phone number YOU WILL likely be SIM swapped at some point and the scammer would be able to gain access to your X account. 

(US cell carriers are primarily being targeted but have seen Canada/EU as well)

3/  Linking a phone number for 2FA is simply not acceptable. 

X allows people to add Security Keys or an Authenticator App instead. 

I usually tell people to buy Yubico security keys (one primary & one backup) as I have seen instances where people accidentally back their Authenticator App up to the cloud. 

Buy directly from their website and not a reseller. Also make sure to write down back up codes in case you ever lose the device.

Keep in mind if you ever deactivate your account 2FA will be removed and will have to be added back.

4/  Good cyber hygiene means not reusing emails or passwords across different services (should be obvious)

Options for email include Skiff, ProtonMail, or setting up your own custom domain

You should always assume your info has been leaked online through breaches at some point

5/  In recent weeks we have seen teams such as Certik fall victim to 
Calendly phishing links where accounts pretend to be journalists from Forbes or Bankless. 



If you ever accidentally authorize access make sure to revoke access immediately.

settings -> security and account access -> connected accounts

As a general rule I do not click links from random DMs or connect my X account to apps (verify the URL and permissions the app is asking for at a minimum)https://x.com/zachxbt/status/1743260567060787424

6/  If your X account ever becomes compromised from a SIM swap contact X support immediately: 

I have found it typically takes 3-5 business days on average to hear back from support. 

It is good to create a support ticket with the email that was associated with your account before or official website domain. Make sure to keep your case number handy.

I would also message @premium or create a post in  to help escalate your ticket so you hopefully hear back faster.

Understand they receive a large amount of requests so keep your message straight to the point.x.com/i/communities/…

7/  Alert your community through Discord, Telegram, etc. 

The slower you are to react means more potential people fall victim to the phishing posts.

Also consider offering compensation to victims as most ATOs are preventable and likely due to your own negligence.

8/  Once access is restored by support immediately check if the scammer delegated access to an alt account and remove them if so. 

settings -> security and account access -> delegate to make sure the scammer did not delegate access to anyone.

EX: This is how the Algorand Foundation CEO was compromised twice.

9/  If you follow the tips shared above this will put your team in a good position to limit potential attack vectors.

While this may not be new info to most people it unfortunately has to be said again since teams are lazy and compromises still keep happening.

Another thing to keep in mind is there are companies who larp and say they can recover your account for an unreasonably high fee (they are likely useless).

description: @zachxbt: 1/ Throughout this year I have been monitoring someone who has withdrawn 11,200+ ETH ($25M) from Tornado Cash and spent the majority of it on Magic The Gathering (MTG) trading cards. Here’s my analysis of w...…
fullText:
1/  Throughout this year I have been monitoring someone who has withdrawn 11,200+ ETH ($25M) from Tornado Cash and spent the majority of it on Magic The Gathering (MTG) trading cards. 

Here’s my analysis of where the funds went and what the potential source of funds could be.

2/  This person has withdrawn 110 X 100 ETH from Tornado to 11 addresses. 

After they would:
1)  Wrap the ETH
2)  Transfer WETH to new address
3)  Unwrap the WETH
4)  Transfer USDC to MTG broker

(this is a strategy used to trick KYT at exchanges)

3/  After USDC was sent to a MTG US based broker that accepts crypto

How did I find the broker used?

1)  Instagram username was same as on OpenSea

2)  Directly contacted a few MTG sellers the broker interacted w/ on-chain

Broker address
0x80462101b56cb4125c645ff299d3e20c1d908c02

4/  After contacting MTG sellers were where things became interesting. 

-buyer was spending millions on starter decks, alpha sets, sealed boxes

-buyer seemed to be overpaying by 5-10%

-buyer sent crypto up front and broker met up IRL with seller

-buyer was unknown to seller 

-said the broker has limited crypto knowledge (likely does not know about Tornado)

*seller names will be kept private for their safety*

5/  The funds also go to various deposit addresses at Kraken, Bitpay, and Coinbase. 

0x34e158883efc81c5d92fde785fba48db738711ee
0x3a43ac6baf1fa6bdbc966dbdfe26cf545131898e
0x85cb90db50608a950858e023509d6a7fa289e212
0xbfe6def287c402114d39d0156e17fda79efff4d2

6/  Where do I think these funds could have originated from? 

To start I began looking at the top Tornado depositors who were active throughout the past year using a Dune query created by @bax1337 

-Anubis (12400 ETH) 
-Cashio (11500 ETH)
-Uranium (11303 ETH)

Using timing and multi denomination reveal heuristics I arrived at the thesis that the funds potentially originated from the $50M Uranium Finance hack that occurred in April 2021.

Anubis had previously potentially been solved however and Casino did not deposit enough ETH earlier in the year to match the withdrawals of this person.

7/  Here’s some of my rationale behind it being the Uranium hacker:

Oct 4, 2022 the Uranium hacker deposited 5.01 ETH total to Aztec (privacy tool) at 22:03 UTC

0xd332be2c39de1f4ecd4ef6ce23ae826906a8a144ebbfefb9cf2a74c7d320f563

Just 2 hours later at 00:15 UTC on Oct 5 this person received 2.7 ETH from Aztec

0x2b8745157bd13cb7aa76444af67e7de0bf0b288bff50886b599942a17e0e298c

8/  In March 2023 the Uranium hacker deposited 52 X 100 ETH to Tornado & this person received 52 X 100 ETH

March 6 & 14: Uranium Hacker deposits 52 X 100 ETH to Tornado

March 7 & 15: Our person withdrew huge volumes from Tornado

After they finished the Uranium hacker deposits more in May

9/  While my analysis could be incorrect I find it very suspicious that this person:

-spends 8 figures on MTG 
-is overpaying for MTG 
-shields identity through broker who likely does not know what Tornado is
-receives $13.2M from Tornado post OFAC while in the US
-uses WETH method to obfuscate source

description: @zachxbt: 1/ An investigation into the Canadian scammer known as Yahya for their involvement in 17+ SIM swaps which resulted in more than $4.5M stolen. 2/ Yahya’s job was to do lookups on X/Twitter accounts using his...…
fullText:
1/  An investigation into the Canadian scammer known as Yahya for their involvement in 17+ SIM swaps which resulted in more than $4.5M stolen.

2/  Yahya’s job was to do lookups on X/Twitter accounts using his panel so the scammer Skenkir could get US targets for SIM swaps. 

As compensation for his work Yahya would receive a % of the proceeds stolen from each attack. 

EX: Here is screenshots of Yahya showing off tools

3/  In July 2023 Yahya & HZ (scammer who had assets seized by FBI) scammed someone named Amir for $250k (136 ETH) after pretending to sell access to his panel. 

In doing so this revealed Yahya’s wallet address:
0x7da33a98247b584b0070355881be9085126b53e1

7-2-23 at 3:04 UTC payment sent
7-2-23 at 3:20 UTC confirmation 

HZ context:

4/  Using Yahya’s address from the Amir payment is where things get interesting. 

By reusing the same address for the panel scam as for the SIM swaps payments this connected all of his activity. 

In total he received 390+ ETH ($720K) to his address from 17+ SIM swap attacks.

5/  EX 1: @GutterCatGang 

July 7, 2023 a GCG team member was SIM swapped and bc the phone number was attached to their account they gained access and posted malicious links. 

This lead to $720K+ in losses after @digging4doge lost 6 figures worth of NFTs. 

Yahya received $250K for his efforts.  See messages below of where he discusses selling stolen assets. 

0xb964b8f02bab49a40dae06736de748b5c6801c1258df2d362aa1063522b0fbdf

6/  EX 2: @Bitboy_Crypto 

June 10, 2023 Bitboy Crypto was SIM swapped with the same method as GCG leading to $950K in losses. 

Ironically one of the scammers involved named Smoke ended running off with the funds himself scamming the other scammers.  


https://twitter.com/bestvideosofct/status/1717173391340294361

7/  EX 3: @SlingshotCrypto 

June 19, 2023 Slingshot was attacked in the same way leading to $36K in losses. 

Yahya received $9.7K for lookups.
0x7d5746c30bef5cb32b55f8257b82984f347b154deb64766eb115ae2196e4bb30

8/  EX 4: @PleasrDAO 

July 19, 2023 they targeted PleasrDAO core team member Jamis, who had suffered a traumatic brain injury not long before. 

Sadly this lead to $1.3M in losses. One of larger victims lost $807K MAGIC. 

Yahya received $144K
0x2c15a71d261522a7624c286257a3c1dcaca1281a9250bcffeb66bc85ef946004

9/  Yahya has spent thousands of dollars buying watches and unreleased Juice WRLD songs such as: Dark Tints, Biscotti in the Air, Oxy in the Dark, No Jumper.

10/  He shows little remorse for his actions when confronted by people.

“Like bro its a lot of money they offering just for lookups”

11/  Before getting in involved with illicit activity Yahya was a contributor to Benzinga for SMM using the name Yahya M*******

I am not sure why you would want to take that risk doing lookups for SIM swaps if everything connects back to you.

12/  I hope some of this can help serve as a  basis for action against these scammers who have harmed many people in the space with SIM swaps and phishing. 

Chat records and other things have been archived and will be happily provided to victims.

description: @zachxbt: 1/ What happened to the funds from the @slope_finance $4M hack? Here’s my analysis tracing the latest movements in 2023 and where the stolen funds ended up going. 2/ TLDR: Slope Wallet (founded by Leal Cheu...…
fullText:
1/  What happened to the funds from the @slope_finance $4M hack? 

Here’s my analysis tracing the latest movements in 2023 and where the stolen funds ended up going.

2/  TLDR: Slope Wallet (founded by Leal Cheung) was hacked in August 2022. After the hack their entire team disappeared. 

Multiple Solana community members I reached out to confirmed this. 

In May 2023 whoever ran the account fell for a prank and accidentally made a tweet.  
https://twitter.com/slope_finance/status/1559581264813780994

3/  In March & August 2023 the hacker was active laundering funds off of Solana from the original theft addresses.

SOL Consolidation address: 
4JfXWXd2aenLncrdpJcryX8kZVJUSHyGPnc6HSyhA2Es

4/  The hacker used instant exchanges (Binance nested) to transfer funds from Solana -> Ethereum

Using timing analysis I was able to find the destination addresses as they were reused each time.  

ETH destination address
0x9372e8fa0286e8aed82fe9f897bba1ae549f3f02
0xf59880f2e434a0a7e598bbeab27ab2301cbc4f08
0x67208653edc4f8bed74bdacc84f7251d7255e0e1
0xb22b4f480a717505d37944a953bb60b4f741acfa

5/  From the ETH addresses above  fun were deposited to Tornado Cash.

Tornado deposits (322 ETH total)
March 9 - 8 X 10 ETH
March 27- 6 X 10 ETH 
July 14 - 6 X 10 ETH
Aug 15 - 1 X 10 ETH, 2 X 1 ETH
Aug 23 - 11 X 10 ETH

6/  I then demixed the Tornado withdrawals. For some reason the Slope hacker withdrew funds to only one address.

0x25b31cfaedc95a83b72f72c087c3745f8b00a299

7/  From 0x25b funds were then bridged to TRON via SWFT. 

On TRON the USDT was transferred to a number of active addresses with prior USDT transaction history. 

Typically these types of addresses are small services/OTCs used to cash out funds P2P.

@SlowMist_Team released their analysis last year and reached a similar conclusion of the Slope Hacker using an OTC on Tron.

8/  It’s unfortunate to see the Slope team took no responsibility for the hack and made little efforts to make things right. 

Their X/Twitter and Telegram is full of angry victims asking about progress on the investigation.

It’s unlikely these victims see anything returned due to how this was managed. 

List of wallet addresses related to the hack:

Update: It also seems founder of @slope_finance (Leal Cheung) went and created a new project @zkme_ immediately after the hack. 

Considering the fact he abandoned the entire community and did little to resolve the Slope hack I would avoid this project entirely.

fullText:

description: @zachxbt: 1/ Part 1 of an breakdown into how @TraderNJ1 @PetaByteCapital have deceived multiple projects by leveraging the names of other influencer to obtain free tokens from CBOT and BABYSHIB to dump on followers u...…
fullText:
1/  Part 1 of an breakdown into how @TraderNJ1 @PetaByteCapital have deceived multiple projects by leveraging the names of other influencer to obtain free tokens from CBOT and BABYSHIB to dump on followers undisclosed.

2/  Recently this audio clip of Trader NJ surfaced asking for a % of CBOT token supply to shill with Peta, and others saying they will make tweets saying a project is the next 10-20X. https://twitter.com/gregcryptoegg/status/1698318363221532984

3/  EX 1: BABYSHIB 

In Telegram messages provided by the BabyShib team it shows a conversation with Peta/NJ discussing marketing for 3.5% of the supply 

They even claim to have “no intentions of selling anything anytime soon” 

NJ/Peta provide both wallets to receive tokens

4/  Aug 25 Peta & NJ began receiving BABYSHIB as compensation and began heavily shilling on Twitter shortly hyping it up. 

Aug 30 they began unloading all of the tokens selling $115K+ over next few days even though it is a lowcap. 

They currently do not appear to hold BABYSHIB. What happened to not selling anytime soon?

5/  Peta claims “I don’t do paid promotion and never have” yet these Telegram messages and BABYSHIB received as compensation says otherwise.

6/  In this message received from the BabyShib team they outlined how NJ & Peta had not followed the original deal as they did not onboard SZ or XO to market the project, did not make YT videos, and sold almost immediately after.

7/  EX 2: CBOT 

In a recent post by the CBOT dev they revealed there was a similar deal to BABYSHIB where NJ/Peta asked for a % of the supply and promised to onboard influencers as well. 

Peta eventually left the chat & NJ supposedly deleted the message history

8/  I reached out to Icarus who then provided me with five wallet addresses the CBOT team had sent tokens to. 

Similar to before majority of tokens were dumped in 1-2 weeks after.

9/  Nowhere in their shill tweets did either Trader NJ or PetaByte disclose they were compensated with a % of the token supply to promote it.  



https://twitter.com/bestvideosofct/status/1700891895503671725

10/  It’s clear their strategy here is deceptive here as they

>act like they were not compensated by the projects
>act like they bought into the project themselves
>use other influencers names to get a % of the supply
>lie to projects and play victim when caught

Part 2 will include Trader SZ & XO and their involvement.

11/Sources:

BABYSHIB wallet addresses

0x1dc24c42874f7a0dd190b230f461eef69253c270

0x39d037ddd4bef37268fbaf4578b7a1f8f52bfb49
0x6ce1b4d16436dda74582572fdfe5d01afa2b391e

Here is part 2 https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1700920283798839443

3/6  Here is an example of one of the fake requests made with the stolen identity for the project @arbitrum

fullText:

2/6  Most alarming to me is how the WorldCoin team has boasted about how many users they have.

fullText:
1/  An investigation into the Canadian phishing scammer known as Soup (Dan) who has helped steal millions in assets by attacking the Discord servers of projects like @Orbiter_Finance @PikaProtocol

2/  Soup creates fake @decryptmedia websites and poses as Luke Hamilton (a real Decrypt employee)

He works with other scammers to approach team members of crypto projects to trick them into joining a fake Decrypt Discord server in an elaborate attempt to steal their Discord token

0x21bc8046245880e9d3ec5ed808048a93518e933a  https://t.co/yml7nrxkKf

https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1678384917657296898

4/  On 5-30-2023 the Pika Discord server became compromised after scammers gained access to a team account. 

This scammers posted a malicious link in the announcements channel helping them steal $220k+

0x2162b6caa174b90416090bd4a87e7d08f7027c48

0xd7d1d6692e0612a632a78fab5a82e9c767d518bf

0x4566c86A5E8080a237EC59251C96D781c6E3d504  https://t.co/afbSbj3wlf

description: @zachxbt: 1/ An investigation into the YouTuber turned phishing scammer Blue (Jack) who has worked with Monkey Drainer and other drainer services to steal more than $1.5m 2/ Before Blue (Jack) began scamming in 2021 ...…
fullText:
1/  An investigation into the YouTuber turned phishing scammer Blue (Jack) who has worked with Monkey Drainer and other drainer services to steal more than $1.5m

2/  Before Blue (Jack) began scamming in 2021 he amassed an audience of more than 122k subscribers by uploading gaming and trolling videos to YT.

3/  A leaked post from Blue’s personal Snapchat shows the ENS address cool-breeze.eth which purchased BAYC 8668 in October 2022.

0xC9a4Fe3DA7fb38e57fa14B273f05f190b535E395

4/  Looking at the funding of this address is where things get interesting.

I observed inflows to cool-breeze.eth of more than 85 ETH (~1/3 of the stolen funds) directly from 8+ addresses involved with Monkey Drainer phishing scams.

Monkey is best known to have stolen  $24m.

5/  On Discord Blue flexed his BAYC purchase and would share his methods scamming. 

The second and third screenshot is from an internal Discord server where Blue would instruct his workers.

6/  Unfortunately Blue was robbed of his BAYC in late 2022 after allegedly scamming Monkey out of his cut from a phishing scam. 

0x20428b7e16bdbe9b59478de05694d9aeb0d4393cfba65d46318e600581d7df55

7/  This did not deter Blue as he soon began scamming again shortly after this setback with new ENS addresses.

The stolen funds have been spent on luxury watch, car, shoes, Roblox items and gambling on Stake. 

All this was flexed from his personal Snapchat.

8/  Blue specializes in spamming on Twitter with verified accounts and two of his recent thefts include stolen assets from @g13m and @ystrickler while working with two different drainers.  https://t.co/48ruH7mC9v


https://twitter.com/g13m/status/1675944577956474886

9/  July 3, 2023 @g13m was phished for 61.5 ETH and 93.3K USDT (~$213k) by a fake Doodles site. 

Blue received 49 ETH and 74K USDT for himself after the drainer fee. 

Blue’s address
0xa27b1d84b928059d78b7fdad9f7142d84e8689b4

10/  After Blue commissioned a video with his old BAYC and his other alias making fun of LoveMake losing his funds.

11/  Another recent theft includes yancey.eth who had a Milady & Squiggle phished (~12 ETH)

After the drainer fee Blue received 10.3 ETH to his ENS purplelobster.eth

Blue moved the stolen funds to 0xad where $300k currently sits
0xad83776f97d956e4c9b33fd180e43910a8cb7db4

12/  It’s disgusting to see these phishing scammers show zero remorse and spend the funds on tasteless items.

Additional information of Blue (Jack) has been shared with multiple of his victims to eventually help bring legal action.

fullText:
1/  It’s unfortunate I have to make this thread but I am being sued by MachiBigBrother for an article I published in June 2022.

Today Machi filed the defamation lawsuit. The lawsuit is baseless and an attempt to chill free speech. I intend to fight back & defend free speech.

2/  This is a classic David & Goliath story.  My understanding is that Machi is very wealthy. I am not. He is using his money to try silence me. I’m asking for your help so this doesn’t happen & the truth survives.

3/  I am creating a donation address to assist with legal costs associated with the defense of this matter, which could easily exceed $1m USD. 

All leftover funds will be returned on a pro rata basis to contributors.

4/  Donation wallet address 

ETH: 0x6eA158145907a1fAc74016087611913A96d96624

(All EVM Chains are accepted and stablecoins are preferred)

5/  It is sickening to see it come to this but I knew one day this would happen as the price of telling the truth is sometimes people dislike what you say. 

I have always kept my research as objective as possible and let the data and historical record speak for themselves.

6/  A copy of the article at issue can be found below if you are unfamiliar and wish to give it a read. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1537435280176623616

description: @zachxbt: 1/ In recent weeks influencers have given @souljaboy praise for new NFT projects in hopes of clout. In reality he has been one of the most shameless promoters in the crypto space. In my research I...…
fullText:
1/  In recent weeks influencers have given @souljaboy praise for new NFT projects in hopes of clout.

In reality he has been one of the most shameless promoters in the crypto space.

In my research I observed 73 promotions & 16 NFT drops done by him. Many of these were scams.

2/  Let’s walk through some examples of the most egregious shills for scams done by Soulja Boy in the crypto space.

As being US based he is supposed to be following FTC guidelines yet has ignored them. 

He was recently charged by the SEC for Tron & was sued for Safemoon.

3/  EX 1: RapDoge

On July 19, 2021 Soulja Boy Tweeted out: “let’s pump $RAPDOGE to $.000001 and let’s get all our friends in on this are you with me lilyachty” 

In the following hours the project rug pulled after receiving shills from Lil Yachty and Quavo as well.

4/  EX 2 & 3: Orion & The Life Token

These two projects used cancer and suicide prevention charities as a means to pump the price. 

Within one of month of the shill Orion rugged and Twitter was deleted.

The Life Token was abandoned in early 2022.

5/  EX 4: SaferMars 

As probably his most infamous shill, SouljaBoy accidentally left his compensation details in the promotion tweet and left it up before noticing. 

The project soon rug pulled after and he deleted his tweets to remove evidence.

6/  EX 5: Flokinomics

On Oct 9, 2021 Soulja Boy tweeted about a scam that blatantly paid for media features and claimed it was created by Elon Musk.  

Eventually the twitter & website were deleted

7/  EX 6: ParrotsDAO 

Soulja Boy tweeted “Get yo money up and stake!”

In next 24 hrs the project rug pulled and he deleted all evidence once again.

8/  Soulja Boy has dropped many different NFT collections. In 2021 alone I found 9 total collections created by him. 

One of them called SouljaBoyNFT advertised many forms of utility yet the website is offline.

9/  Then just in April 2023 SouljaBoy has dropped 7 different NFTs.

The first included a 3D NFT which used the Ferrari & Nike logo. Within a few hours this was blocked from OpenSea for IP infringement.

It seems he has yet to reimburse the buyers for it.

10/  Some of the recent NFT drops by him have advertised utility such as exclusive unlocks for his upcoming blockchain video game and other member only benefits. 

He has continued to create more NFTs disregarding buyers of previous collections.

11/  During the last crypto bull run Soulja Boy was charging up to $12k for Instagram & $10k for Twitter promotions.

Using this number these promotions would have netted him around $730k + funds from his various NFT collections I found.

(this is just an estimate) https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1644030130686394368

12/  It’s abundantly clear Soulja Boy will do anything to make money regardless of whether or not it hurts his fans. I suspect there are more examples than what I found. 

I believe he should be made an example of as legal action does not seem to deter his actions.

Warning for the people who bought  @thevaporware (VAPOR) as it is directly tied to the scammer JKDAO who rugged two previous projects.

His public address immediately received 3.8% of VAPOR supply from the deployer & $44k (22.8 ETH) has been sold so far with 1.7b VAPOR remaining.

For additional context about his previous scams:

0xaADa6E18924155bF140E6C9C26Ed7aB8301d338f https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1518295137419415553

Update: JK quickly dumped another 1.1b tokens for 5.6 ETH ($10.9k)

description: @zachxbt: 1/ Ever wonder who connected projects with the celebrities and influencers that promote crypto scams? Here’s a breakdown on one of the people doing it known as Raichu (Ryan). 2/ A quick visit to Raichu’s we...…
fullText:
1/  Ever wonder who connected projects with the celebrities and influencers that promote crypto scams? 

Here’s a breakdown on one of the people doing it known as Raichu (Ryan).

2/  A quick visit to Raichu’s website shows he claims to have helped sell out over $15m for projects through marketing and promotions. 

What he doesn’t say however is that many of these projects were outright rug pulls or pump and dumps.

3/  Luckily for us he publicly documents a lot of these sketchy promotions on his Instagram account by posting them as highlights.

4/  EX 1: Here Raichu shows off a promotion he helped organize with rapper Lil Xan for the project Baller Ape Club (BAC)

In June 2022 the BAC founder was charged by the US DOJ for wire fraud and international money laundering after the project turned out to be a $2.6m scam.

5/  EX 2: Here he works with the NFT with Lil Xan and Dillon Danis to connect them with a NFT project called Crazy Lemur Club.

Within just weeks after launch the team rug pulled with $320k. The founder was even outed by myself of being behind over $5m+ of phishing scams. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1587480151847305222

6/  EX 3: For this one Raichu worked with rapper Rich The Kid for the project Ancient Cats Club. 

The team quickly rugged shortly after launch stealing over $1.7m. The team of this project eventually launched other NFT scams like Bored Bunny and Rich Dwarves Tribe. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1522222303253340161

7/  EX 4: Raichu worked with The Game and Dillon Danis to promote Baby Dinger token another scam.

8/  EX 5: Here Raichu worked with Antonio Brown to promote the NFT project Vault of Gems. 

Just weeks after launch the team then disappeared with over $1m. It was later revealed this team was behind a number of NFT scams in a 57 page document.

9/  The full list of projects is exhaustive and I was able to find 50+ examples of scams or pump and dumps publicly shared with many of the influencers and celebrities not disclosing it was a promotion.

10/  It’s unfortunate to see him flex making money this way after leaving people in financial ruin.

At what point does it become malicious when working with so many sketchy projects.

How many buzzwords can these sketchy  projects use? 

>domain registered Jan 16, 2023
>twitter registered Feb 2023
>fake followers/engagement
>immediately launched token
>team/insider tokens unlock early on

And they claim to have over 2M users…

Somehow this was listed Day 1 on Bybit and Bitfinex.

Would be weary of anyone you see promoting $GPT as it shows they do close to zero due diligence. https://twitter.com/crypto_ed_nl/status/1637754206269440001

description: @zachxbt: 1/ A thread on the Canadian phishing scammer known as Chards who has stolen millions spending it on luxury cars, watches, NFTs, & social media usernames. 2/ A few weeks ago the Discord user madman#9528 beca...…
fullText:
1/  A thread on the Canadian phishing scammer known as Chards who has stolen millions spending it on luxury cars, watches, NFTs, & social media usernames.

2/  A few weeks ago the Discord user madman#9528 became active in the Bored Ape Discord showing off their ENS address madman.eth as well as BAYC 7941 & 6716.

Linked to the ENS however was where things got particularly interesting.

3/  For example back in September 2022, madman.eth accidentally linked their address back to a phishing attack they were responsible for where BAYC 4651 & 18 other NFTs were stolen for 115 ETH.

Luckily in this instance I was able to help recover 33.5 ETH of the funds.

4/  Also directly tied to madman.eth is payment to the scammer serpentau.eth (Lock) who is responsible for the recent Azuki, MutantHounds, Chimpers, & AKCB Twitter compromises.

5/  And a 17.5k USDC transfer tied to HZ/Chase who recently had their assets seized by the FBI as a result of their phishing scams. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1621576193152634881

6/  Interestingly madman.eth also purchased a $13.5k Rolex watch from the same watch seller HZ & Diablo got theirs from. 

0x4c52189b5d99076f3f876577eda9c836e9504b8821b248e3080314336ade3021 https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1580607777533493248

7/  Madman.eth is linked to the theft of BAYC 5153, receiving 54 ETH just after the Kumaleon Discord server was attacked. 

Fake_Phishing7900:
0x62d480A2771796493eEBb03965da676C97F398C9

8/  Madman then began flexing his purchase of an Audi R8 as well as bragging he’s crashed 3 X BMW M4s in the BAYC Discord.

Pretty disgusting considering they were purchased with stolen funds.

9/  I used the pics of R8 which then brought me to the Instagram  account cold (dave) who purchased the same R8 at a dealership in Canada. 

Their Instagram account also consisted of wrecked BMWs just as mentioned on Discord. https://twitter.com/bestvideosofct/status/1627649315345772544

10/  His Instagram consists of him flexing bottle service at a nightclub in Toronto with friends and pics of his iced out watch.

11/  His Discord account shows some of the of the social media accounts owned by him.

Twitter: @choke @madman
Instagram: liar, cold, blood
Discord ID: 1044739071743893605

12/  It’s disgusting to see phishing scammers continuously ruin the lives of people in the space with zero remorse meanwhile they flaunt the stolen funds across socials media. 

I hope this information will help victims such as Snarls, Eurion, and countless others.

13/  If you find my research interesting or if you were a victim of Chards yourself please consider donating to my wallet:

zachxbt.eth

0x9D727911B54C455B0071A7B682FcF4Bc444B5596

Hi @retlqw since you deactivated your account after I messaged you.

I've traced addresses back to your account from the @Platypusdefi exploit and I am in touch with their team and exchanges.

We’d like to negotiate returning of the funds before we engage with law enforcement.

I’ve reviewed your transaction history across multiple chains which lead me to your ENS address retlqw.eth 

Your OpenSea account links directly to your Twitter and you liked a Tweet about the Platypus exploit.

Your old Tweets seem even more suspicious.

Loyalist: $4m stolen from over 400 victims 

zachxbt.mirror.xyz/chj355oHn5PcRI…

This graph visualizes everything from the theft of NFTs to laundering the stolen funds to a personal address and multiple exchange accounts.

Here is a breakdown of the unique victim accounts per day and thefts by collection.

More than 1700 NFTs were observed to have been stolen by Loyalist since early 2022.

Last year I posted a thread about @cryptomanran @crypto_banter. 

Shortly after they engaged with me to highlight inaccuracies in the thread and to work together on setting a new standard for their business and the space.

First Ran clarified some of the addresses were indeed shown to not be owned by them. He states any mistakes made were early on before their code of conduct was implemented.
 
We then worked on a framework to strengthen internal controls and prevent mistakes from repeating.

fullText:
Last year I posted a thread about @cryptomanran @crypto_banter. 

Shortly after they engaged with me to highlight inaccuracies in the thread and to work together on setting a new standard for their business and the space.

First Ran clarified some of the addresses were indeed shown to not be owned by them. He states any mistakes made were early on before their code of conduct was implemented.
 
We then worked on a framework to strengthen internal controls and prevent mistakes from repeating.

After reviewing their current processes, they shared changes made in recent months, & I made  recommendations (some they already implemented in recent months)

They’ve also added a method to contact myself on their website if you suspect any wrongdoing.

I’m pleased to see their initiative and glad they have been receptive to my ongoing efforts to improve the space. 

They also demonstrated their effort to change their content to be more responsible, and make changes that will enhance confidence going forward.

fullText:
1/  A thread on two verified accounts @radoko @Fitz_lol that popped up out of nowhere in the past few weeks and all the different NFT rug pulls they’ve created in that time.

2/  Both accounts popped up suddenly tweeting about NFTs with NFT pfps at the end of December 2022.

SocialBlade does a great job visualizing the huge jump in fake followers.

It’s likely the account was either sold or stolen.

3/  Shortly after they began making cringe Tweets botting the engagement and promoting 6+ rug projects they create.

4/  How do we know they created them? 

Well if you start with @radoko pfp you will see it’s MAYC 5998 owned by: 

0x00c69661c3b084baa98a69da64ae3a30441662e5

5/  Then we go to a rug @Fitz_lol “created”

The deployer of the contract for the project is 
 0x77e796bd46245c1b5bb96df025328c614404caa5

6/  Mapping it out we can see it’s 1 hop away from @radoko public wallet as well their other rugs @TrippyFrogsNFT @FatNutzETH 

In total these projects the scammer created netted them ~40 ETH in the span of a few weeks.

7/  And then looking at their Tweets that leads to two more additional rugs @350pass @proof_christmas 

All of the projects follow a similar trend of being funded via FixedFloat, created with BuenoArt, & low supply. 

Before moving onto the next scam.

8/  Here we have all the victims who minted the various rug projects.

9/  Stop following, cotweeting, or replying to random NFT accounts just bc of their pfp, followers, and engagement. 

Somehow I share 10+ mutuals with both accounts.

List of addresses used in thread:
chainabuse.com/report/d5053e3…

Update: Messages from Discord show the original owner sold access to the account unknowingly to a scammer and gets 20% of the funds from the rugs.

description: @zachxbt: 1/ A thread on how @jyxdi profits off the work of an Fiverr artist while implying the art is her own to launch NFT projects such as @spiritgates. Let’s dive in. 2/ Recently I found out...
fullText:
1/  A thread on how @jyxdi profits off the work of an Fiverr artist while implying the art is her own to launch NFT projects such as @spiritgates.

Let’s dive in.

2/  Recently I found out that Jyothee supposedly an “award winning artist” was buying pieces for $25-50 off an artist named Sajol Hassan from Bangladesh on Fiverr.

This first began in May 2022 with 60 pieces being commissioned since then.

3/  She would post the art on Instagram/Twitter and lead people on that she created these pieces on her own. 

Even launching an NFT project Spirit Gates that brought in $376k (281 ETH) in Sep 2022.

Let’s walk through some examples.

4/  EX 1: Here was a piece she claimed to have created

Here is a message with the real artist Sajol who sent Jyothee the files via Fiverr

To make it more believable she would ask Sajol for the sketches so she could post them online

5/  EX 2: She tweets out “cookin up some 🪄”

Here is the message of Sajol sending her the sketch and final version of the piece.

6/  EX 3: Here is the cover photo on OpenSea and Twitter for Spirit Gate. 

Once again designed by Sajol.

7/  EX 4: Here she talks about a work in progress. In reality Sajol sent her the sketches beforehand.

8/  EX 5: Sajol also created the base model for the Spirit Gates NFT project

9/  I reached out to @jyxdi to ask about this. 

She claims that he was credited on the website in October (oddly weeks after the project minted?)

I reviewed the website archive and found no evidence of this. 

web.archive.org/web/2022100909…

10/  She also claimed to have mentioned Sajol in a Twitter space yet conveniently the Twitter space doesn’t seem to be recorded.

There is currently no mention of Sajol in the projects Discord, Twitter, and OpenSea either.

11/  I reached out to Sajol and he said he’s asked for credit 4-5 times in total.

Right before posting this I reached out Jyothee who says he will be visible soon (we will see)

12/  It seems strange to me the art she is posting on her personal Instagram and Twitter is art she didn’t create? 

If you look at the comments many people assume she did create them.

13/  At a minimum buyers of Spirit Gates and her art should be fully refunded and/or the real artist be fairly compensated.

Here is the link to the artist Sajol if you wish to commission their work on Fiverr:

fiverr.com/sajol_artworks…

Update: Here is a interview where Jyothee talks about Spirit Gates and specifically takes credit for the promo art drawn by Sajol. https://twitter.com/bestvideosofct/status/1604234770577346561

Update: She went private and deleted her response thread.

You can read the archive here:

archive.ph/W13k6

My replies:

archive.ph/VC8jR

description: @zachxbt: 1/ I have seen a ton of misinformation being spread on Twitter and in the news about the FTX event so let me debunk the three most common things I’ve seen “Bahamian officials are behind the...…
fullText:
1/  I have seen a ton of misinformation being spread on Twitter and in the news about the FTX event so let me debunk the three most common things I’ve seen

“Bahamian officials are behind the FTX hack”
“Exchanges know who the hacker is”
“FTX hacker is trading meme coins”

2/  The first clue that 0x59 was a blackhat and neither Bahamian officials or FTX team was when 0x59 began selling tokens for ETH, DAI, and BNB and using a variety of bridges so crypto couldn’t be frozen on 11/12. 

Also look at the slippage on some of these swaps

3/  The fact 0x59 was dumping tokens and bridging sporadically was very different behavior from the other addresses who withdrew from FTX and instead sent to a multisig on chains like Eth or Tron. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1591475248700223488

4/  The next clue was when 0x59 on 11/12 sent 3168 BNB via BSC to 0x24 and then Huobi. 

0x24 has very sus behavior on-chain using sketchy services such as  a small Russian exchange “Laslobit”

5/  This behavior completely differs what was said about the Debtors moving assets to cold storage or Bahamian government moving assets to Fireblocks.

6/  Another clue was when earlier today the blackhat began to sell ETH for renBTC and then used Ren bridge. 

The funds will likely be sent to a mixer at some point in the future.

7/  A lot of misinformation was being spread about “Kraken or other exchanges having found the attacker” when in reality it was the FTX group securing assets to a multisig on Tron. 

Kraken had been used to since the FTX hot wallet was out of TRX for gas https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1591444221738754050

8/  This matched the behavior for 0x97 multisig which had also been funded via a CEX as well.

9/  The withdrawals to these multisigs also matched what Ryne Miller (FTX GC) had said at the time. 

This took places hours after the initial 0x59 withdrawals.

10/  Lastly I see tons of people saying “the FTX hacker is trading meme coins” when in reality it is someone spoofing transfers to make it look they are. 

medium.com/etherscan-blog…

11/  Please triple check who you get your info from. Many people are using the FTX event to appear knowledgeable for engagement when in fact they have zero clue what is going on.

Seems to me there’s two separate groups involved with the FTX/FTX US funds fiasco

BlackHat

>took losses from slippage

description: @zachxbt: 1/ A short thread sharing a recent success of mine where I helped @Snarls4651 recover $51k after being phished for 20 NFTs including BAYC, BAKC, Veefriends, XCopy, and more back in September. 2/ On Septembe...…
fullText:
1/  A short thread sharing a recent success of mine where I helped @Snarls4651 recover $51k after being phished for 20 NFTs including BAYC, BAKC, Veefriends, XCopy, and more back in September.

2/  On September 21st I received an notification about a suspicious sale for BAYC 4651. 

I noticed the victim had all their valuable NFTs transferred from their wallet where the scammer began to sell them for ~110 ETH.

3/  I monitored the stolen funds & saw they were soon transferred to an exchange. I was then able to reach out directly to the team and share a report of the incident i had created resulting in $51k frozen

Sadly 50% of the stolen funds from the attack had already been moved prior

4/  To give some perspective this all had to be done in <1 hr as there is a very small window for success

After I guided him through the next steps of the process. With the help of his local police and the exchange, $51k from the attack was recently returned

5/  My work is possible bc of donations from the community so if you support my efforts please consider donating. 

zachxbt.eth

0x9D727911B54C455B0071A7B682FcF4Bc444B5596

fullText:
1/  Over the past 24 hrs ~700 ETH ($1m) has been stolen by the phishing scammer known as Monkey Drainer. 

They recently surpassed 7300 transactions from their drainer wallet after being around for only a few months.

2/  The two largest victims over the past day include 0x02a & 0x626 who collectively lost $370k from signing transactions on malicious phishing sites operated by monkey drainer such as the ones below.

3/  Victim 0x02a lost 1 BAYC, 1 CloneX, 36.k USDC, & 12 other NFTs worth in total around $150k.

Here’s a screenshot obtained from inside the scammers group chat and the transaction as well.

Chainabuse report (am advisor):
chainabuse.com/report/87260e3…

4/  Victim 0x626 held over ~$6.2m worth of crypto at the time but managed to only lose $220k.

Luckily they rejected the other transactions.

Chainabuse Report:
chainabuse.com/report/a6ea274…

5/  These victims are just two of many who’ve had their funds stolen by Monkey.

The total number stolen easily surpasses $3.5m with that number rapidly increasing by each day.

Two main wallets
0x9FC8265F2b376084423A1A348a89Ecd894a9d106

0x84527B5949D479c879b8Dd71cd8f79048CdF6fB8

6/  Please be extra diligent before visiting unknown sites, connecting your wallet, and signing transactions.

Am still away on holiday for another week or so but wanted to get this post out as my bot notifications have been blowing up from this.

1/  Investigating the phishing scammer Diablo and how he’s linked to @ezu_xyz @nounsdao @MoonLanderzNFT Shadows & more attacks which all tie back to an iced out rolex he purchased with illicit funds.

2/  Background: Diablo/Josh works under other scammers like HZ, Two1, or Chards messaging potential victims and then gets a small cut of whatever is stolen from that attack.

3/  On July 28 Diablo ($olana) sent $10k USDT to the watch seller to buy an iced out rolex. 

Here are some of the messages from that Discord server by Diablo.

4/  In case you’re wondering what the iced out rolex looked like here is a video Diablo had shared with friends about the purchase. https://twitter.com/bestvideosofct/status/1580606467387711488

5/  Interestingly enough this was the same watch seller HZ/Chase had used previously (see old thread) so now we already had the sellers ETH address. 

Here is the USDT payment for the watch.

0xa694586fc6444ce57c47a73f947760fbad3189345fa96117d36f515515322001

6/  When you map out the address Diablo used to pay for the watch you see how it’s directly tied to the Nouns DAO, Moonlanderz, Shadows, Ezu

Address:
0xf8e402f12917900afcd8daeda97b23aae524db9f

Breadcrumbs report (an investor):
breadcrumbs.app/reports/3275

7/  Here are additional screenshots of people complaining how those addresses were used for the MoonLanderz, Flurs Pass, Shadows Discord attacks.

description: @zachxbt: 1/ Time for an investigation into the @beeple Twitter hack which resulted in $450k+ stolen, where those funds are now, and tracking down the three people responsible. Let’s jump in. 2/ On May 22 @beeple Twi...…
fullText:
1/  Time for an investigation into the @beeple Twitter hack which resulted in $450k+ stolen, where those funds are now, and tracking down the three people responsible. 

Let’s jump in.

2/  On May 22 @beeple Twitter account became compromised and the attackers posted links to two different phishing sites which drained various NFT & cryptos for ~$450k (225 ETH)

Attackers:
0xF305F6073CFa24f05FF15CA5b387DD91f871b983

0xcad7fc974F61A08ADEF110D1BA446fa5b5B5Bb27

3/  How did his Twitter account become compromised? 

In a previous thread I shared how Cam Redman (SIM swapper) sold Twitter panel access to scammers which gave them access to accounts regardless of an account owners security. 

Video (patched Aug  2022)
 https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1562473638992850947

4/  Beeple recovered his account hours after the attack and the scammers proceeded to deposit the stolen funds into Tornado Cash. 

Where did the funds go? https://twitter.com/beeple/status/1528380635081068544

5/  On 5-22 at 7:20 pm UTC the attacker sent 3 X 10 ETH and 6 X 1 ETH (36 ETH) from 0xf30 to Tornado Cash.

Just <2 hrs later 0x664b withdrew 3 X 10 ETH and 6 X 1 ETH (36 ETH) from Tornado and sent the 36 ETH to FixedFloat.

0x664B589a3042F55222Bb5981d47Ca6a1eD4745F4

6/  On 5-22 at 4:17 pm UTC the attacker sent 100 ETH, 6 X 10 ETH, 3 X 1 ETH (163 ETH) from 0xd15 to Tornado. 

Just minutes later 0x2fc withdrew 100 ETH, 6 X 10 ETH, 3 X 1 ETH (163 ETH) from Tornado.

0x2Fc55F49783Caf72628eb3fe0380671ed9A57684

7/  Interestingly the 0x2fc address belongs to a scammer known as Two1/Youssef. How do we know? 

Well first the 0xcad address from Tweet 2 was funded by the same address  who attacked the @OthersideMeta Discord.

In the attached thread I go in-depth to how it leads back to Two1 https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1569323567942180866

8/  Well there’s another way 0x2cf leads to Two1. 

After receiving the Beeple funds from Tornado, Two1 sent the funds to the same 0x5bc address he flexed on Twitter back in June.

0x5bC1792E002447eAf18Dd3e7cf231B01299f6d8a

9/  Early July, Two1 sent the ETH to wealthyindividual.eth before depositing it all into Tornado. 

Consistent with previous behaviors Two1 only left the ETH in Tornado for ~2 hours before withdrawing it all from Tornado to:

0x2FA030d5a64FDA8e69048a2A6DB4C534889E3BA2

10/  It was moved then moved to 0xe84 where 152/225 ETH from the Beeple hack currently sits. 

0xE84D4E6451119f49F24f13cAf13FBda331C2245f 

This address has since been flagged for phishing by the @etherscan team after my first Two1 thread was posted.

11/  On 5-23 at 3:27 am UTC the attacker began to send 2 X 10 ETH, 5 X 1 ETH (25 ETH) from 0x5c25 into Tornado.  

Just minutes after each deposit the attacker once again withdrew 2 X 10 ETH, 5 X 1 ETH (25 ETH) from Tornado to 0x702

0x702De97D9030B83ce04CD3BC5509CBeAd42Ec41d

12/  If you look closely you’ll noticed 6 X 1 ETH was withdrawn from Tornado in total from that address. 

Where does that extra 1 ETH come from? 

Well it turns out they also attacked the @webaverse Discord & sent to the same 0x702 wallet as the Beeple hack funds.

13/  The 25 ETH from the beeple hack was then moved from 0x702 to 0xf20 where it currently sits. 

0xf2000037a148ea53d3f9c24f3b8607c847b60091

14/  I’ve shared how Cam & Two1 were both involved in the hack but there’s one more. 

That person is @bandage aka ShinePranked or Shayan. He is the one that connected Two1 with Cam for the attack and got a %. 

After my preview Tweet yesterday he deactivated his Twitter (lol)

15/  Also after my preview Tweet  Lockvert (Lock) a phishing scammer who’s friends with Two1 & Shayan DM’d me on Twitter to ask details about this thread. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1567919208801120258

16/  Let me briefly summarize everything:

Cam sold Twitter panel access to Two1 & Shayan which enabled them to hack @beeple. After the attack Two1/Youssef deposited the funds into Tornado but withdrew the funds immediately in similar amounts so it was easily traceable for myself

17/  163 ETH of the stolen Beeple funds sit here: 

0xE84D4E6451119f49F24f13cAf13FBda331C2245f

25 ETH of the funds here: 

0xf2000037a148ea53d3f9c24f3b8607c847b60091

The rest of the funds were sent through FixedFloat and would likely need to be subpoenaed for additional info.

18/  All my work is possible because of donations so if you like my investigative threads such as this one please consider donating to my ENS or sharing this thread! 

0x9D727911B54C455B0071A7B682FcF4Bc444B5596

zachxbt.eth 

Thanks to @beeple for making this sick 1 of 1. https://twitter.com/beeple/status/1577135985728839681

19/  Sources:

Chainabuse report with relevant addresses (am advisor):

chainabuse.com/report/e5c78a7…

Breadcrumbs (am investor):

breadcrumbs.app/reports/3245

description: @zachxbt: 1/ An investigation into how crypto influencer @TheCryptoLark promotes low cap projects to his audience just to dump on them shortly after. Let’s dive in. 2/ Before I go over eight different examples let me...…
fullText:
1/  An investigation into how crypto influencer @TheCryptoLark promotes low cap projects to his audience just to dump on them shortly after.

Let’s dive in.

2/  Before I go over eight different examples let me share how I found Lark’s wallet through two different ways:

a) listed as a donation address in an old YT video from Nov 2017

b) from an NFT Lark tweeted out

Larks Wallet:
0x468cB54a3821d8b0129C42Ea6ADf12748d97fD98

3/  Example 1: $UMB

a) Feb 9, 2021 at 4 pm UTC Lark receives 62.5k UMB at launch
b) Feb 9, 2021 at 8:16 pm UTC Lark makes a tweet about the launch
c) Over next few hours Lark dumps all of the 62.5k UMB for over $136k USD.

4/  Lark tweets about the first two projects ( $DOWS $SHOPX) to launch on the Paid Network Launchpad and states “both will moon hard”

Well let’s see what happens with those!

5/  Example 2: $DOWS

a) Mar 1, 2021 at 4:23 pm UTC Lark receives 96k tokens 
b) Mar 1, 2021 at 10:42 pm UTC Lark Tweets about the project launching in 16 hours
c) At launch Lark dumps 24k DOWS for over $56k USD. 

DOWS has since been abandoned by the team since Dec 2021. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1564366005618749441

6/  Example 3: $SHOPX

a) Mar 31, 2021 at 3:31 pm UTC Lark receives 120k SHOPX
b) Apr 1, 2021 at 12:32 am UTC Lark Tweets about the project
c) Meanwhile Lark is in the process of dumping all his 120k SHOPX for over $435k USD (finishes on 4/2)

7/  Example 4: $BLES

a) Mar 26, 2021 at 3:03 pm UTC Lark receives 16k BLES
b) Mar 27, 2021 at 10:09 pm UTC Lark Tweets about BLES
c) Over the next hour Lark dumps $28k USD worth of BLES
d) Over a few day period he dumps all of the 16k BLES for $103k USD

8/  Example 5: $PMON  

a) 3-24-2021 one week before TGE Lark Tweets about PMON
b) 3-30-2021 the day before launch Lark includes a segment about PMON
c) 3-31-2021 at launch Lark receives 10,000 PMON
d) Over next few hours he dumps all of his PMON tokens for over $313k USD

9/  Example 6: $BMI

a) Jan 30, 2021 at 1:23 pm UTC Lark receives 20k BMI
b) Jan 30, 2021 at 9:55 pm UTC lark begins selling all of his BMI for $42k USD.
c) Jan 30, 2021 at 10:57 pm UTC he tweets out he is “super bullish” even though has already dumped his tokens by then.

10/  On November 9, 2021 Lark Tweeted out he doubled his position in BMI. 

While it is entirely possible he has an alt wallet from his main wallet it is clear he did not double his position from his main wallet. 

(unfortunately can’t ask him since he blocked me months ago)

11/  Example 7: $XED

a) Dec 28, 2020 Lark Tweets about XED 
b) Dec 30, 2020 at 7:49 am he Tweets again about the launch later that day
c) Dec 30, 2020 at 2:09 pm UTC he receives 100k XED
d) Dec 30, 8:08 pm UTC he starts dumping all of the tokens; finishing in 1.5 hrs for $34k

12/  Example 8: $APY

Lark drops a YT video for “100x low cap gems” on Feb 12 2021 then proceeds to dump his tokens that same day. 

While there are many more Polkastarter & Paid Network Launchpad projects he’s shilled I think it’s pretty clear what is going on here.

13/  A common theme you’ll find between launchpad projects is awful tokenomics which is why a ton went to zero before the bear market even began. Also no decent VCs really ever touch them.

14/  Participating in seed rounds & sharing projects you genuinely like is completely fine as long as it’s done in a transparent manner.

This is not the case as Lark has a pattern of dumping his discounted launchpad bags right after shills across YT, Twitter, & newsletter.

15/  Lark claims he doesn’t get paid but ask yourself what value might he possibly add to these launchpad projects besides shilling it to his followers?

His compensation is being able to get large allocations for projects with awful tokenomics that he can dump in a shady manner.

16/  Collectively between the 8 examples shared that’s already $1.2m+ of low caps dumped and for many of the projects it was just the first unlock.  

Between all the YouTubers I’ve dug into this is easily one of the more egregious instances of dumping on followers I’ve seen.

If you find my research insightful please consider donating to my ENS, Gitcoin, or sharing the thread.

zachxbt.eth

0x9D727911B54C455B0071A7B682FcF4Bc444B5596

gitcoin.co/grants/5039/za… https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1575494380072611847

Update: Lark responded so let’s address his defense. I’ll post the screenshots since many of you are blocked too.

https://twitter.com/thecryptolark/status/1575633350701686785

He claims it’s nowhere near enough to effect price when he is dumping 6 figures of tokens on a micro cap after aggressively shilling?

He says he purchased them the same way you would too when he got huge allocations and all of these projects had scammy vesting and were acquired at a price way lower than public allocations.

Nowhere in my post for the examples does he disclose

He talks about all the coins here and is funny since he always hyped the coin up before launch then sold immediately.

Of course the coin pumps on listing when the price starts at like $100k initial market cap.

He claims to like these projects yet dumps instantly after shilling

He acts like normal ppl get into these sales when in reality so few do

He blames the APY sale on another YouTuber/team failing yet made a 100x video then?

Its hilarious he blames the bear market for my post when these projects went to zero months before the bear market began

It’s a bit disingenuous to say these are “ridiculous allegations” when they’re actions taken from a wallet you own and Tweets & videos made by yourself.

Please stop looking for guidance from crypto influencers who only wish to exploit you for their financial gain.

Update on Lark https://twitter.com/leomschwartz/status/1589997684332511232

description: @zachxbt: 1/ Since Dec 2021 we’ve seen 600+ Discord servers compromised & 12+ NFT related Twitter accounts hacked as well. This has resulted in millions of dollars being stolen. Welcome to part 2 of tracking down the...…
fullText:
1/  Since Dec 2021 we’ve seen 600+ Discord servers compromised & 12+ NFT related Twitter accounts hacked as well. This has resulted in millions of dollars being stolen.

Welcome to part 2 of tracking down the people responsible. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1562473638992850947

2/  As a TLDR to those who missed the first part of the investigation, I uncovered that Cam (who previously SIM swapped $37m), sold Twitter panel access to scammers known as HZ & Popbob enabling them to hack 12+ NFT Twitter accounts.

https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1562473793968570368

3/  Safe to say horror (HZ/Chase) was not too thrilled with my thread calling him out for the Twitter hacks.

4/  Why would he ask to take them down? 

Few remember that prior to the Twitter hacks HZ has been also responsible for the majority of Discord attacks working with Popbob and Two1 

Back in April 2022 I mapped out their known wallets with over $3.9m in them at the time. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1518600321953681408

5/  As of a few weeks ago HZ/Chase began flexing on Twitter an iced out Audemars Piguet watch.

Well where did he buy that watch? https://twitter.com/horror/status/1562585366502469634

6/  I realized HZ likely paid in crypto due to the nature of how he obtained the funds. 

I then asked around a few mutual friends who sell watches. This lead me to a watch seller who recently sold that AP watch for $47.5k USDC.

7/  Inside the Discord server for the seller were two flex pics by the buyer (HZ) with the first made on 08/19/22. 

What looks similar? Well the success pics inside the Discord from the buyer were the EXACT same watch and sweater as posted on Twitter by HZ.

8/  I then chatted with the watch seller who was completely unaware of what had transpired. After learning what HZ has done they confirm it’s the same watch and have me look into the address HZ paid with.

0xdc25df861f979a175bfe4f3737d1562d45cdc5cd

Watch payment:

9/  I then map out the flow of funds.

The address HZ used to pay the watch seller $47.5k was DIRECTLY funded by multiple addresses used to scam people with hacked Twitter accounts such as @deekaymotion @Zeneca_33 @ezu_xyz @JRNYclub

(highlighted in yellow below)

10/  Just 1-2 hops away from this address HZ is tied to the @BoredApeYC @nansen_ai @AnataNFT @LACOSTE @TheParallaxHQ @cheebsnft Discord attacks and to @nounsdao @franklinisbored Twitter account hacks.

More attacks aren’t displayed for the sake of space.

11/  On June 28 the JRNY Club Twitter was hacked. 

In total HZ received 73.56 ETH ($114k) from the scam to the same address used to pay for the watch. 

TXID:
0x8879d3f03028954c406484ae818b59511d75c3eaaea8f815fe374db7aa387077

12/  On July 14 DeekayMotion Twitter account was hacked. 

In total HZ received 23.11 ETH ($36k) from the scam to the same address used to pay for the watch. 

TXID:
0x8bc3cdf25a31476f685aee8bd004868f90356a1219e0ae1879b9f353640ed379

13/  On July 19 Zeneca’s Twitter was hacked.

In total HZ received 20.36 ETH  ($31.7k) from the scam to the same address used to pay for the watch. 

TXID:
0x2ae1482e97f56c5e88a54e8457b4a1c6199341eb6deffe0fb4b865e474a5c375 

(note HZ is tied to the 333 club Discord attack too)

14/  Some of the Discord attacks like the BAYC Otherside and 333 Club ones HZ is tied to stole $300k+ worth of NFTs with each attack. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1518600329071415310

15/  Since December 2021 @NFTherder has tracked at least 600 Discord servers that’ve been compromised (thanks for calculating this number). 

Here is his latest report for August 2022: https://twitter.com/nftherder/status/1565325049787293700

16/  After months and months of tracking their group it’s nice to know one of the main perpetrators (Chase Senecal) for NFT/crypto phishing attacks has been identified. Others in his group like Popbob have yet to be caught

Hopefully at some point victims will take legal action

17/  The majority of the stolen funds haven’t been spent & sit in wallets like:

$1.27m
0x9971e3efc26ce470f806c725518e51dee118202e

$730k
0x5f7a8e85071e992473a016529b86c6bf0c4e50a4

$828k
0xe84d4e6451119f49f24f13caf13fbda331c2245f

Some is spent on OG usernames like @skull @horror

18/  Sources: 

Chainabuse report (am advisor):
chainabuse.com/report/19be733…

Breadcrumbs report (am investor):
breadcrumbs.app/reports/2851

Archive:
archive.ph/BSBAW

19/  Hundreds of hours go into reports like this. If you appreciate my research please consider donating to my wallet. All my work is possible because of it.

description: @zachxbt: 1/ Cameron Redman is the alleged person responsible for the hacked NFT Twitter accounts over the past few months Does the name ring any bells? Well it should bc in February 2020 he SIM swapped a...…
fullText:
1/  Cameron Redman is the alleged person responsible for the hacked NFT Twitter accounts over the past few months

Does the name ring any bells? Well it should bc in February 2020 he SIM swapped a single person for $37 million worth of Bitcoin & Bitcoin Cash

Time for a thread

2/  On February 22, 2020 Josh Jones  was sim swapped for ~60k BCH & 1547 BTC 

BCH victim address
qzumak2rvxksjgkjuxe2fe5jxatktlsnhy5sthr5p7

BTC victim address 1
bc1qd0hveqwqu9h3x8flfq560hlyk9mptf3j2p89gg

BTC victim address 2
bc1qrwhh74sv88gzq6qgpz5u55u00w2lqprw30ke94

3/  Immediately following the attack the Redman laundered the 60k BCH with hundreds of transactions in small amounts to centralized exchanges (CEX).

This chart visualizes the movement of the 60k BCH with the majority ending up at two exchanges.

4/  With the 1547 BTC stolen, a small portion was sent to CEXs but the majority was deposited into both Chip Mixer and Crypto Mixer.

5/  On Nov 17 2021 Hamilton Police (Ontario, CA) formally charged Redman for the attack after working with FBI & US Secret Service

They seized $5.4m worth of crypto. The rest of the $31.5m still remains missing to this day

(he was underage at the time) 
hamiltonpolice.on.ca/news/arrest-ma…

6/  Since May 2022 we’ve seen popular NFT Twitter accounts hacked such as @beeple @jenkinsthevalet @nounsdao @deekaymotion @Zeneca_33 @frankdegods @KeyboardMonkey3 @franklinisbored & more

The majority of these accounts all had 2FA on leaving people confused how this might happen.

7/  The hacked Twitter accounts lead to millions of dollars worth of crypto stolen in total. 

This table shows a rough estimate for the amount of crypto stolen  with each account in the Tweet above.

8/  This lead to a post on the forum marketplace SWAPD by the user “Antihero” advertising a Twitter panel. 

The prices to use the panel varied from $30k to $300k paid in crypto.

9/  On Twitter at July 29th 2022 antihero emerged with the name “Cam” on an account inactive for 14 yrs.

On Instagram he obtained the same username too.

10/  On Instagram Redman posted a selfie of himself posing in front of mirror and also outside of a shopping center.

11/  I zoom in and then look up the location of “Sunway Dental” 

What do you know it happens to be in Missauga, ON very close to the Hamilton Police station in the city where Redman had been previously arrested in Nov 2021.

12/  If you’re still not convinced here’s more messages of him referencing Canada.

Prior to being charged for the SIM swap Redman had been also known by the aliases “Cream” “4k” “lucky” and for leaking unreleased Juice WRLD songs. https://twitter.com/FallenLeaks/status/1461050441832312835

13/  Here’s two more posts Cam/Antihero made on SWAPD. Another person refers to the seller for the panel as Cam. 

Archive:
archive.ph/ABvZy

14/  Remember in Tweet 9 Redman began to eventually resell access to the panel. 

On June 26 2022 Cam received a 230 ETH + 20 ETH payment for lifetime access to the Twitter panel. 

0x0214ee97e9d828e1e6aa49a85b89982a7dceacc7

15/  Just a few hours after the payment @nounsdao Twitter was hacked.

The scammers address had direct exposure to the JRNY club Twitter hacker, Nansen discord attack, and other Discord attacks.

Source:
chainabuse.com/search/address…

16/  Who bought the Twitter panel access from Redman? Well it was the scammers known as HZ/Chase and Popbob. Here’s HZ flexing panel access to @Serpent (a security researcher)

17/  HZ + Popbob flexing Franklin and Deekay being hacked.

18/  It’s still unclear as to how Redman gained access to the panel to make elevated requests & reset passwords. As of now it appears the method stopped working

It’s wild someone can SIM swap a person for $37m, only return $5.4m, & go back to their old ways w/o serious jail time

19/  Thanks for making it this far. Feel free to share this thread with others. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1562473638992850947

Update: seems they’re upset with this thread

If you appreciate my research/work please consider donating to my ENS or Gitcoin.

description: @zachxbt: 1/ After $3.4m, lie after lie, and the team disappearing for months here’s a deep dive into the rug pull @winterbearsnft by @0xnushi @OphidianDev @gumptionbear 2/ The project launched in Sept 2021 releasing...…
fullText:
1/  After $3.4m, lie after lie, and the team disappearing for months here’s a deep dive into the rug pull @winterbearsnft by @0xnushi @OphidianDev @gumptionbear

2/  The project launched in Sept 2021 releasing their 1st collection Winter Bears (WB) netting the team 291 ETH.

Later they launched a 2nd collection Summer Bears which was airdropped in Dec 2021

A third collection 3D Bears was launched in Dec 2021 netting the team 369 ETH.

3/  Originally advertised in the roadmap was at 25% minted the team would giveaway a Pudgy Penguins NFT. At 50% minted they would giveaway a Cool Cat NFT. 

In October 2021 this was removed by the team from the website with the NFT giveaway never happening.

4/  The team engaged in deceptive marketing where they sent the influencer ArtChick 5TH to sweep the floor to create fake hype

Nushi had WB holders raid AC tweet so it would make it look like the raid was the reason for AC purchase

This pushed WB to top of the hourly rankings

5/  Since launch the team falsely advertised WB as a ticket for Art Club DAO & would offer: revenue share for holders, free airdrops, exclusive NFT drops (said 2 big NFT projects lined up), & more. 

This was first said in Sept 2021. It’s now Aug 2022 and nothing has happened.

6/  In Oct 2021 WB said they partnered with PieDao and holders would be rewarded with KPI tokens

Nushi claimed they would “manage an index fund that could be worth many millions” 

Holders reached out to PD and it was revealed WB team never even put up a proposal or told PD

7/  A major selling point for buyers was 10% of the proceeds or $40k donation would go to a polar bear charity

It was not until April 2022 Nushi addressed this & claimed they now couldn’t donate

As of now there’s no evidence of monetary donations for Polar Bears

8/  One of the roadmap items was an airdrop for the second collection Summer Bears, to holders. 

They advertised there would be SB staking & were also working on something to provide utility and value to both SB & WB. 

As of Aug 2022 there has yet to be any utility for either.

9/WB team also advertised holders would receive a PixBear airdrop for each WB owned for a partnership with Pixlverse

In Feb 2022 this changed when the WB team announced 3DBears would get PixBears instead

Why the change? Well prior to the 3D bears had been struggling to mint out https://twitter.com/winterbearsnft/status/1489010789134606343

10/  Since then the partnership with Pixlverse has been terminated. This was bc the WB team failed to communicate with the Pixlverse team & didn’t meet deadlines.

Can’t say I’m too surprised with this result given the WB team has delivered on almost nothing since launch.

11/  WB team also originally advertised the 3D Bears would be metaverse compatible, wearables, animated, and P2E gaming

Instead they bought flat renders of the 3D Bears which couldn’t be rigged for animation and were not Metaverse compatible.

12/  In April 2022 the 3DBear artist who was contracted said the WB team was lying about it being metaverse compatible

“They’re not incapable they’re crooks” 

A leaked convo with the artist from Dec 2021 shows WB team was fully aware they didn’t pay for what was needed.

13/  This chart details where the funds from the WB / 3DB mint & royalties went. The majority of funds still currently sit in the three multisigs. 

A small portion of funds were also sent to Gemini, Coinbase, and Binance.

fullText:

2/  The project was first announced on December 11th 2021 with a supply of 4499 NFT and mint price of 0.2 ETH.  

The roadmap advertised metaverse events, staking, future collections, incubator, and large giveaways.

3/  RDT had @jasonderulo @NeYoCompound @freddurst @smoss heavily shill the project to their audience on Instagram and Twitter

Socials for the project have been dead since January and discord broken/deleted. The team did however complete one of the giveaways before abandoning.

5/  A majority of the funds were transferred away to Binance & FTX accounts directly after minting out.

6/  Interestingly both of the Binance accounts that received a portion of the funds also are connected to three other rug pulls:

description: @zachxbt: 1/ An investigation into how @GoGalaGames @crypto888crypto rugged people for hundreds of thousands of dollars with The Orbs NFT drop. 2/ In December 2021 @GoGalaGames first announced its “The Orbs” drop, a ...…
fullText:
1/  An investigation into how @GoGalaGames @crypto888crypto rugged people for hundreds of thousands of dollars with The Orbs NFT drop.

2/  In December 2021 @GoGalaGames first announced its “The Orbs” drop, a collection of 3333 generative NFTs that would launch on February 13th 2022 in collaboration with musician @bt & influencer @crypto888crypto. https://twitter.com/bt/status/1470943199350906890

3/  Collectors could obtain an Orb by burning three “888 Inner Circle” NFTs for one Orb NFT (888 Orbs reserved) or by purchasing one in a Dutch auction (2445 Orbs reserved)

4/  Between the months of December and February 13th drop many collectors acquired three “888 Inner Circle” NFTs to burn for an Orb. 

The floor for IC NFTs fluctuated between 1-1.5 ETH per IC so many people had spent 4+ ETH to acquire three.

5/  In the days leading up to the February 14th DA, public outcry built up due to the high floor price of Dutch auction and the fact 888 had recently done two other NFT drops. 

11.11 ETH -> 8.88 ETH DA w/ 2445 Orbs. This would’ve netted the team $64m-80m if all had sold out. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1492844852261892100

6/  Due to the backlash @GoGalaGames @ForTheBux & @bt held a livestream hours before the sale to announce they would modify the conditions of the drop removing the 8.88 ETH floor

This sounded good in theory but what about all of the people that spent 4+ ETH to acquire 3 ICs? https://twitter.com/gogalagames/status/1492934281781342208

7/  Gala then made this promise during the livestream:

“If the floor price [of the DA of Orbs] goes below, because the community has spoken, the price that you have spent on your [“888 IC”] NFTs on the burning and all of that stuffs, we will make you whole, we will make good” https://twitter.com/bestvideosofct/status/1551603653970206721

8/  After the livestream IC holders burned their three and the DA commenced. Instead of a floor of 8.88 ETH however the auction actually settled at 0.71 ETH

This netted Gala, 888, BT roughly $5.3m (1874 ETH)

9/  Remember Gala said they would make IC holders whole for any difference? 

Well I spoke with three victims who spent:

Collector A:4.35 ETH 
Collector B: 4.42 ETH 
Collector C:4.07 ETH

This means they were owed 3.64 ETH, 3.71 ETH, 3.36 ETH bc the auction ended at 0.71 ETH

10/  Instead of reimbursing IC holders directly with ETH, Gala/888 decided to give gift IC holders a vial NFT in Mid-March they could redeem for 6 NFT options. 

Each redemption option would be limited to 250 vials (remember there’s 888 vials total)

11/  The first redemption option was targeted for the end of March but was delayed until April. Then it ended up being delayed again to May 3rd.

12/  The first two redemption options (supply 250) for an Jose Delbo artwork happened on May 3rd for two hours.

There was just one issue… neither option ever had any buyers. Anyone who redeemed made back $0

Option 1
opensea.io/assets/ethereu… 
Option 2
opensea.io/assets/ethereu…

13/  The third redemption option then went live on May 7th for two hours and fourth option on May 17th both with a supply of 250.

Landverse (average sale 1.44 ETH) 
opensea.io/assets/ethereu…

Gala Film (average sale 2.65 ETH) 
opensea.io/assets/ethereu…

14/  The fifth and sixth redemption options also went live on May 17th both with a supply of 250.

Galaverse Ticket (Avg sale 1.83 ETH)
opensea.io/assets/ethereu…

Snoop Pass (Avg sale 0.91 ETH) 
opensea.io/assets/ethereu…

15/  After all six vial redemption options it was clear IC people had not been made whole and were at the mercy of Gala/888.

Remember the three people from earlier were owed 3.64, 3.71, 3.36 ETH respectively. 

None of these vial redemption options equated to those amounts.

16/  Holders began to voice their frustrations in the Discord and on Twitter. 

The team found excuses for not making people whole.

17/  In this pic inner circle holders that got screwed voiced their frustration again and one person was muted for 7 days. Another had a team member reply

"isn't your personal therapy discord”

18/  Gala eventually archived the vials channel  because it became “toxic”

I wonder why people might be angry if you said you would “make them whole” then go back on your word…

19/  Because the supply for each redemption option was 250 and there was 888 vials total people couldn’t even redeem for the best option (Gala Film)

The average amount someone would get for redeeming a vial between the 6 options was roughly 1.15 ETH.

20/  Each of the three victims in my example are still owed 1.5-2.5 ETH and that’s just three people…

In total there was 888 vials so consider how many others were screwed over as well.

The price of ETH has also halved since the Orb drop and due to vial redemption delays.

21/  I called @crypto888crypto out last week for going missing from his various projects for months on end after making millions . 

He then proceeded to magically reappear on Twitter later that day and turned off replies. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1549412570880249858

22/  I believe @GoGalaGames @crypto888crypto need to stick to their word of making inner circle holders whole for the difference in floor price & what was spent

At bare minimum IC holders need to be made whole in ETH

It’s awful a large project and influencer can act like this

23/  One other to note is I believe Gala & 888 screwed over BT as well with this by not following along with the promises they made to the community. https://twitter.com/zachxbt/status/1551613858246283264

Update: 888 untagged himself from the thread

Update: Gala responded and still won’t be making people whole as they had promised originally. 

Can’t say I’m surprised. https://twitter.com/GoGalaGames/status/1551643614895394818

Update: This is all 888 has to say

Careful with the Alec Monopoly NFT project minting today.

>Second Tweet after a 9 year break is announcing a NFT pfp project 
>0.5 ETH mint
>Roadmap overpromises
>Anon team

Also: https://twitter.com/cygaar_dev/status/1551327819187294210
